finale.text: 3D Gaussian Splatting开启了实时辐射场渲染的新纪元
finale.sub: 质量与速度的完美平衡,从学术到应用的关键一步

slides.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering
nodes: InstantNGP
Plenoxels
Mip-NeRF360
Ours (6min)
Ours (51min)

callout.text: Real-time rendering (93–135 fps) with state-of-the-art quality (PSNR 25.2)
callout.sub: Training: 6–51 min competitive with fastest methods —— Quality equals/exceeds Mip-NeRF360 (PSNR 24.3) while 1300× faster rendering —— Novel 3D Gaussian representation + differentiable tile-based splatting

slides.title: Related Work
nodes: ① Traditional Scene Reconstruction and Rendering — light fields, SfM, MVS methods re-project and blend input images; neural rendering avoids storing all images on GPU
② Neural Rendering and Radiance Fields — CNNs for blending weights, volumetric ray-marching, NeRFs with importance sampling; Mip-NeRF360 is current SOTA but requires up to 48 hours training

callout.text: 本文方法:仅用 SfM 点云输入,实现高质量结果
callout.sub: 对比:NeRF-like 方法需 MVS 数据 —— 3D Gaussians 作为灵活、可微的体积表示 —— 实时渲染(10-15 fps 交互式,目标实时高分辨率)

slides.title: Point-Based Rendering and Radiance Fields
nodes: NeRF-style volumetric rendering uses continuous density field σ with expensive random sampling along rays (Eq. 1–2)
Point-based methods rasterize unstructured discrete points — flexible geometry, GPU-friendly, avoid MVS artifacts
Neural point approaches (Kopanas et al. 2021/2022) blend N ordered points via α-compositing (Eq. 3) — same image formation model as NeRF, different algorithm
Pulsar (Lassner & Zollhofer 2021): fast sphere rasterization, tile-based sorting, visibility-order rendering
Recent methods (Zhang et al. 2022, ADOP) use MLP for specular effects but require MVS geometry or masks, unclear scalability
Our approach: 3D Gaussians for flexible scene representation, tile-based rendering, real-time speed without neural components

callout.text: 3D Gaussians achieve faster rendering and better quality without neural components
callout.sub: Unstructured explicit GPU-friendly representation vs. NeRF's continuous field —— avoids expensive sampling, MVS geometry, temporal instability —— tile-based rasterization with anisotropic splats and visibility-order α-blending

slides.title: OVERVIEW
nodes: ① 3D Gaussians defined by full 3D covariance matrix Σ centered at point μ (no normals required)
② Gaussian multiplied by α in blending; projected to 2D via viewing transformation W
③ Covariance Σ = RSS^T R^T (scaling matrix S, rotation matrix R) — stored as 3D vector s + quaternion q for independent optimization
④ Gradients derived explicitly for all parameters to avoid automatic differentiation overhead
⑤ Tile-based rasterizer enables α-blending of anisotropic splats with fast backward pass tracking accumulated α values

callout.text: 3D Gaussians with anisotropic covariance — suitable for optimization
callout.sub: Differentiable, projectable to 2D splats for fast α-blending —— Compact representation adapts to geometry of different shapes in captured scenes

slides.title: Adaptive Control of Gaussians
nodes: SfM Points
Initialization
3D Gaussians
Projection / Adaptive Density Control
Differentiable Tile Rasterizer
Image

callout.text: 优化从稀疏 SfM 点云开始,自适应控制高斯密度
callout.sub: 使用快速基于瓦片的渲染器,训练时间与 SOTA 快速辐射场方法竞争 —— 训练后支持多种场景的实时导航 —— λ = 0.2,每 100 次迭代密集化,移除 α < ε_α 的透明高斯 —— 梯度阈值 τ_pos = 0.0002 用于密集化判定

slides.title: Adaptive Gaussian Densification & Fast Tile-Based Rasterization
nodes: Tile-based culling (16×16 tiles, 99% confidence)
GPU Radix sort by view-space depth + tile ID
Per-tile depth-sorted list generation
Thread block per tile: collaborative load to shared memory
Front-to-back traversal: accumulate color & α per pixel
Stop at target α saturation (α→1)
Back-to-front gradient traversal (reuse sorted array)
Store final α at forward pass end for gradient coefficients

callout.text: Tile-based rasterizer enables fast α-blending with arbitrary blended Gaussians
callout.sub: No per-pixel sorting overhead —— Fully differentiable pipeline —— Adaptive densification: clone under-reconstruction, split over-reconstruction —— Approximations negligible at pixel-sized splats

slides.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering

callout.text: Visual quality comparison: Ours vs. Mip-NeRF360, InstantNGP, Plenoxels
callout.sub: 9 test scenes from Mip-NeRF360, Deep Blending, Tanks&Temples datasets —— Non-obvious quality differences highlighted by arrows/insets

slides.title: Quantitative evaluation: our method vs. previous work
nodes: Plenoxels
INGP-Base
INGP-Big
M-NeRF360
Ours-7K
Ours-30K

callout.text: Ours-30K achieves 0.903 SSIM on Deep Blending (highest)
callout.sub: Competitive with M-NeRF360 (0.792 vs 0.815) at 160 FPS vs 0.06 FPS —— 7K iterations already yield 0.770/0.767/0.875 SSIM across datasets in ~6min training
Ablation study: SfM init, densification (clone/split), anisotropic covariance, spherical harmonics all contribute —— Full model on par with SOTA Mip-NeRF360 but trains 35–45min vs 48 hours

slides.title: Limitations
nodes: ① Artifacts in poorly observed regions (other methods also struggle, e.g., Mip-NeRF360)
② Elongated or "splotchy" Gaussians despite anisotropic advantages
③ Popping artifacts from large Gaussians in view-dependent regions (trivial rasterizer rejection)
④ Simple visibility algorithm → sudden depth/blending order switches (addressable via antialiasing)
⑤ No regularization applied to optimization (would help unseen regions & popping)
⑥ Position learning rate reduction necessary for very large scenes (e.g., urban datasets)

callout.text: Method has limitations in poorly observed regions and view-dependent appearance
callout.sub: Anisotropic Gaussians improve quality & alignment vs. spherical (Fig. 10) —— SfM initialization helps (Fig. 7) —— Limiting gradients to 10 Gaussians degrades quality (Fig. 9) —— Spherical harmonics improve PSNR (Table 3)

slides.title: Discussion and Conclusions
nodes: First real-time, high-quality radiance field rendering approach with competitive training times
3D Gaussian primitive preserves volumetric rendering properties while enabling fast splat-based rasterization
Continuous representation not strictly necessary for fast, high-quality radiance field training
~80% of training time in Python; rasterization in optimized CUDA kernels
Building on real-time rendering principles and GPU-optimized software rasterization pipeline architecture provides competitive edge
Potential for mesh reconstruction from Gaussians to bridge volumetric and surface representations

callout.text: First real-time radiance field rendering matching best expensive methods with competitive training times
callout.sub: 3D Gaussian primitives enable fast splat rasterization —— ~80% training time in Python, rasterization in CUDA —— continuous representation not strictly necessary for quality

slides.title: Optimization and Densification Algorithm
nodes: ① Gradient computation for Gaussian covariance matrices (scaling & rotation)
② Iterative refinement loop: sample training view → rasterize → compute loss → backprop
③ Pruning: remove low-opacity (α < ε) or oversized Gaussians
④ Densification: split high-gradient large Gaussians (over-reconstruction) or clone small ones (under-reconstruction)
⑤ Rasterizer design: radix sort splats per frame, 16×16 tile binning for parallel GPU processing

callout.text: Algorithm 1 combines gradient-based optimization with adaptive densification
callout.sub: Gradients derived for quaternion rotation & scaling (Eq. 8–11) —— Refinement alternates pruning & splitting/cloning based on ∇L thresholds —— Rasterizer uses radix sort + tile-based parallelism for real-time rendering

slides.title: Ours-30k achieves best LPIPS scores across Tanks&Temples and Deep Blending scenes
nodes: Truck
Train
Dr Johnson
Playroom

callout.text: Ours-30k: 0.148 LPIPS on Truck (lowest across all methods)
callout.sub: Consistently best perceptual quality (LPIPS) — Tables 7–9 show SSIM/PSNR/LPIPS for Tanks&Temples + Deep Blending; Mip-NeRF360 dataset averages: PSNR 27.58, SSIM 0.790, LPIPS 0.240

script.text: 3D Gaussian Splatting,SIGGRAPH 2023的突破性工作:实时渲染达到93到135帧每秒,质量PSNR 25.2,超越Mip-NeRF360的24.3,但渲染速度快1300倍
相关工作回顾:传统NeRF方法需要MVS数据,训练耗时长达48小时;本文仅用SfM点云输入,目标是实时高分辨率渲染
点云渲染的演进:NeRF使用连续密度场,需要昂贵的光线采样;点云方法GPU友好但缺乏质量;本文的3D高斯避免神经网络,用瓦片光栅化实现可见性排序的alpha混合
方法概览:3D高斯用各向异性协方差矩阵表示,可微分且可投影到2D splat;协方差分解为缩放矩阵S和旋转矩阵R,存储为3D向量s加四元数q,便于独立优化
自适应控制流程:从稀疏SfM点云初始化,每100次迭代密集化,移除透明度低于阈值的高斯;梯度阈值0.0002判定是否需要克隆或分裂
瓦片光栅化器:16×16瓦片剔除,GPU基数排序按深度和瓦片ID,每个线程块协作加载到共享内存,前向后遍历累积颜色和alpha直到饱和
这是9个测试场景的视觉对比:我们的方法与Mip-NeRF360、InstantNGP、Plenoxels的质量差异,箭头和插图标注了非显而易见的细节
定量评估:Ours-30K在Deep Blending达到0.903 SSIM最高分,与Mip-NeRF360的0.815竞争,但渲染速度160帧对比0.06帧;7K迭代版本6分钟训练即可达到0.770 SSIM
这是实时渲染的质量与速度双重突破
消融实验:完整模型平均PSNR 26.05;SfM初始化、密集化策略、各向异性协方差、球谐函数都有贡献;完整模型训练35到45分钟,对比Mip-NeRF360的48小时
局限性:在观测不足区域出现伪影,其他方法如Mip-NeRF360也有此问题;大高斯在视角相关区域产生popping伪影;简单可见性算法导致深度切换;未使用正则化
结论与讨论:首个实时辐射场渲染方法,质量匹配最佳昂贵方法,训练时间竞争;80%训练时间在Python,光栅化用CUDA优化;连续表示并非高质量的必要条件
算法细节:梯度推导覆盖四元数旋转和缩放;迭代循环交替剪枝和密集化,基于梯度阈值判定分裂或克隆;光栅化器用基数排序和16×16瓦片并行
感知质量指标:Ours-30k在Truck场景LPIPS 0.148最低,跨Tanks&Temples和Deep Blending场景持续最佳;Mip-NeRF360数据集平均PSNR 27.58,SSIM 0.790,LPIPS 0.240
